questions.q: Which environment variable must be set to 3 to enable modern backup/restore commands with etcdctl?
questions.explanation: export ETCDCTL_API=3 is required to use modern etcd v3 API features like etcdctl snapshot save and etcdctl snapshot restore.

questions.q: What is the correct order of steps to restore an etcd snapshot on a kubeadm-managed control plane?
options: 1. Update volume mount paths in etcd.yaml; 2. Run etcdctl snapshot restore; 3. Stop kube-apiserver
1. Run etcdctl snapshot restore; 2. Restart kubelet; 3. Update etcd.yaml
1. Stop kube-apiserver; 2. Run etcdctl snapshot restore to a new data directory; 3. Update volume mounts in etcd.yaml to point to the new directory
1. Run etcdctl snapshot restore directly into the live /var/lib/etcd directory; 2. Reboot the node
questions.explanation: Restoring to a new directory and updating the etcd.yaml volume mounts is the safest method. Overwriting a live active directory while the API server or etcd is running can cause fatal corruption.

questions.q: Which of the following certificates and keys are NOT required when authenticating with etcdctl to take an etcd snapshot?
questions.explanation: etcdctl authenticates directly with etcd. It needs the etcd CA certificate (--cacert), client/server certificate (--cert), and client/server private key (--key). The kube-apiserver client cert is not needed for direct etcdctl authentication.
